compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Seabed
- Steam AppID of the game: 583090

## System Information
- GPU: 7900XTX, Steam Deck OLED
- Video driver version:  Mesa 24.1.0
- Kernel version: Linux Zen 6.9.3, but also happens with mainline Kernel
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/sonic2kk/c82c065546cb41172a7bce1138bb6fdc
- Proton version: Proton 8.0-5, Proton 9.0-1, Proton Experimental (20240522)
- OS: Arch Linux with KDE Plasma Wayland 6.0.5

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

[steam-583090.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/15527926/steam-583090.log)

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
When running the game Seabed fullscreen, the window turns black on SteamOS, and contains some artifacts on the Linux Desktop. Running the game in Windowed Mode appears to work fine.

## Reproduction
1. Open Seabed
2. If the game is not fullscreen, press Alt+Enter to make it fullscreen
3. The window will not render correctly
4. Press Alt+Enter to exit fullscreen
5. The game window will display normally.

If the game starts fullscreen, press Alt+Enter to exit fullscreen. Re-entering fullscreen will cause the window to render incorrectly once again.

When running in fullscreen, the Proton log appears to start spamming these two error lines repeatidly:

```
6841.877:0188:018c:err:x11drv:xinerama_get_fullscreen_monitors Failed to get xinerama fullscreen monitor indices.
6841.877:0188:018c:err:x11drv:update_net_wm_fullscreen_monitors Failed to find xinerama monitors at (0,0)-(1920,1080)
```

I am not an expert so I am unsure if these are related.

If the game is not fullscreen, the game appears to run fine from a brief test.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
From inspecting the blame, it seems the code logging this error, and its surrounding code, was introduced in ValveSoftware/wine@bd8ec8f264b5403f90bca6ecb26ae3170995ccd4 ([relevant log line](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/wine/commit/bd8ec8f264b5403f90bca6ecb26ae3170995ccd4#diff-88cf12a6574fc307c8c80271473cab016025709cf6cb0a3e2237070230f5bb71R182)), which introduced support for `_NET_WM_FULLSCREEN_MONITORS` hint.

I wonder if this is an Environment-specific issue. I will update my OP to note that I am running KDE Plasma Wayland.

Out of interest, I spot-checked a bunch of Proton versions, clearing the prefix each time, to see how older Proton versions worked in particular to see how versions before https://github.com/ValveSoftware/wine/commit/bd8ec8f264b5403f90bca6ecb26ae3170995ccd4 behaved. None worked unfortunately, although Proton 6.3-8 and below only show a black screen on my Linux Desktop. 7.0 onwards show minor artifacting.

Also, while these versions don't work, Proton 6.3-8 and below can enter fullscreen much faster with this game. Exiting fullscreen still takes some time though.

I tried in Steam Deck Desktop Mode (the only X11 session I have access to atm), and I was able to get the game to work.

If the game opens fullscreen, or if it opens windowed and is then fullscreened, the following log lines will be in the Proton log:

```
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:dispatch_exception  eax=887601c2 ebx=00000159 ecx=00000004 edx=00000000 esi=007bba14 edi=00000010
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:dispatch_exception  ebp=00b0e704 esp=00b0e698 cs=0023 ss=002b ds=002b es=002b fs=0063 gs=006b flags=00210283
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:call_stack_handlers calling handler at 7BEE6270 code=c0000005 flags=0
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:__regs_RtlUnwind code=c0000005 flags=2
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:__regs_RtlUnwind eax=00000000 ebx=00000000 ecx=c0000005 edx=00b0e6ac esi=00b0e1e0 edi=00b0e230
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:__regs_RtlUnwind ebp=00b0dfbc esp=00b0dfb4 eip=7becee52 cs=0023 ds=002b fs=0063 gs=006b flags=00200206
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:__regs_RtlUnwind calling handler at 7BF9DA00 code=c0000005 flags=2
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:__regs_RtlUnwind handler at 7BF9DA00 returned 1
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:IsBadReadPtr 887601C2 caused page fault during read
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:dispatch_exception code=c0000005 flags=0 addr=7BEDB107 ip=7bedb107
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:dispatch_exception  info[0]=00000000
2911.776:012c:0130:trace:seh:dispatch_exception  info[1]=0000014d
2911.776:012c:0130:warn:seh:dispatch_exception EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION exception (code=c0000005) raised
```

These appear repeatidly while the game is fullscreen and not working.

However, in Desktop Mode (and presumably any at least Plasma X11 session), tabbing out and back in will get the game to work when fullscreen. After this, the above repeated log section goes away,

So perhaps the issues reported above with Xinerama and `_NET_WM_FULLSCREEN_MONITORS` is specific to XWayland (including Gamescope)?

I did not test the Wine Wayland backend yet, but out of morbid curiosity, I will be testing that tomorrow (since these errors from the OP are specific to x11drv in Wine).

I tested the Wayland backend with a build of the unofficial Proton-tkg based from Wine Master (proton_tkg_9.10.r0.g12bbb07c). It indeed started with Wayland (denoted by the alterantive window decorations and incorrect fractional scaling behaviour, and other elements indicative of Wine's current very early Wayland backend) but the game only rendered a black window in windowed mode, and when trying to fullscreen, it attempted to spread across two of my four monitors and then promptly crashed.

I noticed when doing additional troubleshooting, the error block shown on Plasma X11 on SteamOS are also appearing for Plasma Wayland.

I am not sure exactly then what is causing these issues, as Plasma X11 does eventually work, but Plasma Wayland does not. Unsure if Plasma X11 works because it's simply an older version on SteamOS (Plasma 5.27.10 versus Plasma 6.0.5) but still. Given that this issue also affects GameScope on SteamOS Game Mode as well as GameScope nested on my PC, I figure there is something deeper going on here.

<hr>

Some other log lines that may be of interest may be:
- `uiautomation:msaa_provider_GetPatternProvider Unimplemented patternId 10002`
- `oleacc:find_class_data unhandled window class: L"#32769"`
- `uiautomation:base_hwnd_provider_GetPatternProvider 0000000000AD9BE0, 10002, 000000000192F8A0: stub`
As of the new engine update, SeaBed now runs properly with Wine, but the Legacy branch that uses the old engine does not to my understanding.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
The game ships a bunch of .NET Framework dll's that can't be expected to work with Wine Mono due to it being a different implementation and those dll's necessarily relying on implementation details. If I remove System*.dll from the game files, it starts up.

I should be able to update Wine Mono to detect those dll's and refuse to load them so that work-around won't be needed in a later release.

Now that I'm looking at these dll's more closely, the version resource claims they are from Mono. I think the game must be shipping the dll's for use on Linux and Mac (also I am noticing for the first time there is a native version). I guess .NET Framework must ignore these based on filename.

> Now that I'm looking at these dll's more closely, the version resource claims they are from Mono. I think the game must be shipping the dll's for use on Linux and Mac (also I am noticing for the first time there is a native version). I guess .NET Framework must ignore these based on filename.

Hi @madewokherd!
Sorry that I didn't say about this game being Linux native. In Linux there are some problems like the cloud save won't work, I tried to contact the developer about this issue, but I didn't receive an answer, and using proton the game works perfectly (after I remove that DLLs)
Properly fixing this is harder than I thought as described in that issue, it may be a while.
This should work in Experimental now with the 20230817 update.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Gun Metal
- Steam AppID of the game: 267920

## System Information
- GPU: intel hd 3000
- Video driver version: Mesa 23.3.6-1/ LLVM 17.0.6-3
- Kernel version: 6.7.9
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): [report](https://gist.github.com/iodream/21f587c827e63400f174b3e3b084bd5a)
- Proton version: 1711103557 experimental-bleeding-edge-9.0-87026-20240322-pc5407b-we37b544-d28c7c0-v21008d

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

Proton log: [steam-267920.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/14726405/steam-267920.tar.gz)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Menu works but game hangs when loading into the actual mission/level. Also there is no menu or level music, no narrator audio in loading screens. And the intro video is replaced with the proton video testing pattern. 
There is also an additional window created when video is playing, minimizing and taking focus away from actual game.     

I've also tried a more stable Proton 9 and it has the same symptoms. 

Last working Proton version which didn't freeze the game was Proton 6. 

## Reproduction
1. Start Gun Metal
2. Click Load game
3. Click Start 

Observed result: Game freezes
Expected result: game doesn't freeze

following_discussion.discussion_body: Can also confirm the same freezing issue on my oled with Experimental proton, as well as seeing a bunch of test bars for the first intro fmvs.

## System Information
- GPU: RX 6600
- Video driver version: Mesa 25.0.7 / LLVM 20.1.2
- Kernel version: 6.80-90-generic
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/thasreal/e8a0c5512447d53576a7ecccbe55a775)
- Proton version: 10.0-3

## I confirm:
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms 
The game launches with no unsupported graphics card error or anything of the sort, and the intro videos and menu music play just fine for me, but as with the previous person's report, the game hangs when I attempt to launch a mission.
[steam-267920_dxvk.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24351873/steam-267920_dxvk.log)

Launching the game with WineD3D produces similar results, except now the game no longer hangs and progresses into missions (albeit after several ActiveMovie Window windows pop up containing either test patterns or nothing at all). It still lacks the narrator audio mentioned in the aforementioned report, and attempting to play the game with a gamepad makes the player's mecha constantly turn towards the right. This only affects the mecha mode, strangely enough, as the jet mode's controls suffer from no such issue.
[steam-267920_wined3d_gamepad.log.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24351935/steam-267920_wined3d_gamepad.log.gz)
[steam-267920_wined3d_keyboard.log.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/24351940/steam-267920_wined3d_keyboard.log.gz)

## Reproduction
Launch the game and attempt to start a mission, or launch it with `PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1`  with a gamepad connected and attempt to start a mission.

<!--
1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics

issue_report_title: Tekken 7 (389730) freezes whenever i hit another character,Reinstall fixes it once but after that it breaks again
compatibility_report:
Turning off Vibration in game settings seems to fix this
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Tekken 7
- Steam AppID of the game: 389730

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Ryzen 7 5800X 8-Core Processor
- Driver/LLVM version: xf86-video-amdgpu-22.0.0-3.2.x86_64
- Kernel version: 5.18.11-1
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/JeroenMathon/af768570e991b069d09c9d214122985c
- Proton version: 7.x, 6.x  and Expirimental show the same issues

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
Proton logs: https://gist.github.com/JeroenMathon/18e43165760ecbad5f28ac6502100a54
## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Whenever i hit another character in the game or get hit, on the first hit the game freezes, the music keeps playing but the game becomes unresponsive and the video freezes.

Reinstalling seems to fix it for one game, if i start a new session it does the same thing

## Reproduction
Start the game, Open practice or online, Hit or get hit by another character, Game freezes up and becomes unresponsive.
https://youtu.be/kky1f22mTD8 Video of the bug

I have tried nearly every version of proton even custom wine binaries but nothing seems to solve this

following_discussion.discussion_body:
I had the same issue when connected few other controllers to the steam deck. 
Disabling vibration in game menu helps. Game freezes when vibration is about to trigger.

> ed few other controllers to the steam deck.
> Disabling vibration in game menu helps. Game freezes when vibration is

Oh my god this works, i think the reason why proton7.0-2 might work is because vibration does not work in it
This is also working for me.
Playing the first mission chapter starting with the first fight the game instantly freezes and you see a "D-Pad press right". Disabling vibration in Tekkens Game Settings fixes this.

Disabling Vibration fixes this issue, after almost a year im closing this issue since the description does not match the issue anymore, feel free to open a new one for a fix with vibration, im not sure if its fixed in the newer versions if proton, for now im closing this with the following workarround:

Turn off Vibrations in the controller/gamepad settings to resolve the crashes

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Skyrim Creation Kit
- Steam AppID of the game: 202480

## System Information
- GPU: AMD RX 6600 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.2.2/12.0.1 <!-- e.g. Mesa 18.2/7.0.0 or nvidia 396.54 -->
- Kernel version: 5.14.7 <!-- e.g. 4.17 -->
- [System info](https://gist.github.com/fureloka/37aa5df4bc339a8c6eaccc467d6cc5a1)
- Proton version: 6.3-6c
- Proton log: [steam-202480.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/7229455/steam-202480.log)

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Immediately stops after starting, e.g nothing is shown on screen.

Looking at the log, an exception is raised early on
`60767.971:00cc:00d0:trace:seh:dispatch_exception code=6ba flags=0 addr=000000007B01236E ip=000000007B01236E tid=00d0
60767.971:00cc:00d0:warn:seh:dispatch_exception unknown exception (code=6ba) raised`

and it seems unable to find the steam_api.dll file

`60768.311:010c:0110:err:module:import_dll Library steam_api.dll (which is needed by L"Z:\\home\\fureloka\\.local\\share\\Steam\\steamapps\\common\\skyrim\\CreationKit.exe") not found
60768.448:010c:0110:err:module:LdrInitializeThunk Importing dlls for L"Z:\\home\\fureloka\\.local\\share\\Steam\\steamapps\\common\\skyrim\\CreationKit.exe" failed, status c0000135`

## Reproduction

1. Install
2. Press Launch
3. Twiddle fingers while it does its thing
4. Observe Launch button turning green
5. $$$

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Eh, I deserve to get bonked... I had my suspicion when it acted the same on Windows 10, then it hit me that Creation Kit is installed in the same folder as Skyrim. Lo and behold, once Skyrim was installed, it loaded without issues. Oh well, time to test the functionality.

I'll close the issue for now and reopen if I find anything useful.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Same problem here, with EGS copy of it. Happens with both Valve's Proton builds and Wine builds like Wine GE. Vulkan renderer is very slow. Just not 10X but 3X. AMD 7900XTX+ Mesa 23.3.2

I have asked around some folks too ( since EGS recently gave away that game ) and symptoms are:

- Nvidia straight out crashes with Vulkan.

- AMD works ( both RADV and AMDVLK ) but has that huge perf gap. On Windows both renderer options runs equally well.

`WINEDEBUG=+vulkan` and `PROTON_LOG=+vulkan` outputs ( Proton one is from Steam copy from @Blisto91 whom can repro the issue with Steam copy ):

- Blisto's log:

[steam-742420.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13824272/steam-742420.tar.gz)

- My log:

[Cormorant-lastPlay.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13824273/Cormorant-lastPlay.tar.gz)

- From Nvidia user:

[From NV user with crash.tar.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/13824275/From.NV.user.with.crash.tar.gz)


Screenshots that shows Vulkan vs D3D12 perf gap:

![Screenshot_20231230_222806](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/24878978/8de2d523-bcde-480c-83df-390b6ae5a75e)


![Screenshot_20231230_222158](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/24878978/dd4c1eb4-e3c6-4113-a15d-5e1fef417462)

## I confirm:

[steam-218230.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/20928444/steam-218230.log)

- [X ] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [ X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `[PROTON_LOG=1 %command%]()` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Whenever I join fights with ~above 30 people the game will eventually start messing up positional information on my client and models will start flying around everywhere. The desync happens faster the larger the fight is.

#
## Reproduction
Launch the game with this hardware/proton version, find a sufficiently large fight, and wait until models start jittering and eventually flying everywhere.

following_discussion.discussion_body: @GleamingPulsar I think maybe I didn't manage to find a big enough fight but I couldn't get the game to desync. Have you tried newer proton versions (for example 10, or experimental)? if so, do you get the issue there as well?
I have gotten this issue on an older hotfix version, but I will attempt to try again.
The issue still occurs on proton experimental with a fight of around 100 players

The hang behavior is gone with the current Planetside version and the current proton experimental.  Both the fatal hang and the non-fatal 60 second hang after doing a resolution change have stopped happening.
Also observing that the hang is gone

Has anyone else noticed positional desyncs yet in large fights? If not,
what troubleshooting steps could I take to avoid this? I know that the game
struggles with positional data after witnessing some bugs, especially with
poor internet, but this happens in *every* fight above a player threshold
for me, where every model that can move around does (so terminals don't).
Is this a problem with my internet specifically?

On Sun, Jul 13, 2025 at 4:48 PM Thomas Cohn ***@***.***>
wrote:

> *cohnt* left a comment (ValveSoftware/Proton#5295)
> <https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5295#issuecomment-3067293234>
>
> Also observing that the hang is gone
>
> —
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5295#issuecomment-3067293234>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/BFALRWTRVPM345URVGPEDXD3ILAYPAVCNFSM6AAAAABPS4Q4JCVHI2DSMVQWIX3LMV43OSLTON2WKQ3PNVWWK3TUHMZTANRXGI4TGMRTGQ>
> .
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.Message ID:
> ***@***.***>
>

Here to add more context, assuming it's the same issue.

I'm running it through steam-fhs on nixOS (I know, I know, but it shouldn't affect this, it didn't before, and doesn't with other games.  It runs steam in it's own fhs-compatible filesystem).

Sometime around when this sub-issue was posted, a patch came out that reduced this sort of latency... on windows machines, and seemingly made it worse for some linux clients.  Two of my guildmembers using linux seem to report that it works fine.

At least with mine, anywhere you're in a big big fight with a lot (48-96 per side) in only a few rooms, the "server latency" on the scoreboard ticks up slowly over time, not quite exponentially, but with growing numbers, and as long as you're in the area, the RTT will increase steadily to over 20,000ms, and if you move to another part of the map, it will then 'drain', slowly ticking down the latency meter in around 200ms incriments.

That made me explore a few things, now ruled out:
- "only linux... about 200ms added per tick, could it be nagle's?" - turned on QUICKACK, didn't fix.
  - this was a bit of a leap since the game uses UDP, but alas. 
- "maybe it's wifi power saving" no
- "maybe it's my router's flow control interfering with it" no

My theory is something Planetside (Well, daybreak) enabled on their end is interfering somewhere in the WINE network stack, or where it meets the linux one.

edit:
proton: 9.0-4 and experimental
OS: nixos-25.05 and -unstable, 
WIFI pcie card: GIGABYTE WiFi 6E GC-WBAX210, 
wifi controller: Intel Corporation Wi-Fi 6E(802.11ax) AX210/AX1675* 2x2 [Typhoon Peak] (rev 1a)

After a December 16 update to the game planetside has been frequently crashing with an EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION.
Here is an example steam-218230.log from a particularly quick crash.
[gist](https://gist.github.com/stroyan/a847a0b19c6040525522d84e40abe826/raw/bc02937a4a66dbf21190d50126529b7ffdd2040f/steam-218230.log)
The recent crashes related to the December 16 game update do happen with Proton 10.0-3, but do not occur using Proton 9.0-4.

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: We went back
- Steam AppID of the game: 1218250

## System Information
- GPU: AMD Radeon RX590
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 21.1.3
- Kernel version: 5.12.15
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/414n/34891616266f2d4e4489173e8bbee632
- Proton version: experimental-6.3-20210730 

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->
Proton log: [steam-1218250.log.gz](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/6938453/steam-1218250.log.gz)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

The game is playable and can be finished, however there are some surfaces that seem to use videos through `mfplat` that get swapped with the test pattern:

  - the title/pause menu screen: ![we_went_back01](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11655692/128348334-527fa2e6-f063-4920-86e6-8c84baa48a70.jpg)
  - an in-game monitor screen: 
![we_went_back02](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/11655692/128348477-86eea4da-d562-4ade-ba1b-27df29138053.jpg)

Should probably need an entry in #1464 related tables.

## Reproduction

 1. Use Steam Linux beta client
 2. Install and enable "Proton Experimental" for use in "We went back"
 3. Launch the game
 4. The start menu will be "normal" for a couple of seconds, then change to the test pattern

following_discussion.discussion_body:
@alastortenebris this doesn't appear to be a proton issue, I get it to happen on windows as well.
Also, when hotfix is needed by a game it will either be set up as the default proton version of that game or be mentioned in the issue tracker, using it for games at random is not advised as it is seldom updated.
